page: OVHcloud h200-1920 is a high-density GPU-accelerated server designed for intensive computational workloads. It features 224 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz, managed via a KVM hypervisor. The system is equipped with 1920.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 8.57 GB of RAM per core.

The primary hardware accelerator configuration consists of eight NVIDIA Hopper H200 GPUs, delivering a combined total of 1128 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the server includes 8080 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the massive GPU memory capacity and high CPU core count, this server is optimized for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, deep learning model training, large language model inference, and complex data science simulations. The high-density hardware profile represents a specialized tier of resource allocation, trading general-purpose cost efficiency for extreme parallel processing capabilities.
description: OVHcloud h200-1920 is a dedicated GPU-accelerated server designed for massive parallel computing workloads. Running on a KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, it features 224 dedicated vCPUs clocked at 3.0 GHz and 1920.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.57 GB of RAM per core. The server is equipped with eight NVIDIA Hopper H200 GPUs, providing a total of 1128 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by 8080 GB of local NVMe SSDs, while network performance is anchored by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth. One complimentary public IPv4 address is included. This hardware profile is engineered for resource-intensive workloads such as large language model training, complex AI inference, and high-performance data science simulations.

224 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs clocked at 3.0 GHz
1920 GB of system memory providing 8.57 GB per core
